character_voice_profiles.public_register: Easygoing, deflective, charming in a low-stakes way. The voice of a man who has been lying professionally for a decade — warm enough to trust, vague enough to escape. He sounds like someone who genuinely likes people while systematically deceiving them.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Sharp, self-lacerating, dark humor as coping mechanism. The internal monologue of someone who knows exactly how fraudulent he is and finds it both unbearable and grimly funny. Capable of real tenderness toward others but reflexively deflects when that tenderness is directed at him.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Favors compound sentences that pivot midway, often correcting or undercutting themselves. Starts declarative and swerves into qualification or retreat. Under emotional pressure, sentences shorten and become more concrete — he lists physical details when he can't handle abstraction.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Draws from practical, physical, unglamorous vocabulary — dirt, food, money, weather, the body. Avoids heroic or spiritual language even when describing spiritual things. Tends toward specificity about the physical world and vagueness about his own inner state.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No quips that feel written for an audience. No self-aware commentary about his situation ('Well, that's not great'). No smooth one-liners during danger. No introspective speeches where he articulately names his own psychological patterns.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Under pressure, his humor goes from deflective to blunt and his sentences get shorter. He stops lying and starts over-specifying — listing concrete details (the color of a wall, the exact distance to a door, how many coins in his pocket) as if accuracy could protect him. When truly overwhelmed, he goes silent, which is the thing that frightens the people who know him.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Moderate-high. He swears naturally but not constantly — profanity as punctuation, not emphasis. Usage increases under genuine fear and decreases when he's performing charm. His swearing is functional, not theatrical.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Interrupts himself far more than others. Starts a thought, realizes it reveals too much or leads somewhere uncomfortable, and pivots to something else. When he interrupts others, it's to redirect the conversation away from himself.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Frequent. He revises claims in real-time, downgrading them from definitive to provisional. 'It was — I think it was — yeah, it might have been.' This isn't uncertainty; it's the habit of a man who has learned never to commit to a version of events until he knows which version is safest.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Very indirect about himself, surprisingly direct about others. He can name exactly what someone else is feeling or hiding but will not do the same for himself. His observations about other people are his most honest speech.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Low. He gets visibly uncomfortable repeating himself, as if repetition is a tell — a crack in the performance. When he does repeat, it's because something has genuinely broken his composure.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Changes the subject by asking a question, offering a concrete observation about the immediate environment, or making a joke. Rarely says 'I don't want to talk about it' — just stops talking about it and starts talking about something else so smoothly that most people don't notice.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Trails off when emotional, restarts when tactical. Rarely produces a clean, complete sentence about his own feelings. His most finished sentences are his least honest; his most honest are fragments.
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
Look, I've been doing this for eleven years and nothing's ever — well. Something answered. So that's new.
I need to sit down. Somewhere without a corpse.
Yeah, no, I hear you. I just don't — can we talk about this when my hands stop shaking?
Twelve coppers for the ritual, three for the bowl wash, one for the candle. I'm not a charity.
I don't know what I am. I just know I'm not what I was pretending to be, and I'm worse at this.
Sure. Fine. Yeah.
The bread's fine. Just — hand me the other piece. The one without the mold.

character_voice_profiles.public_register: Formal, clipped, morally certain. The voice of a man who spent fourteen years being obeyed and respected, now reduced to commentary from inside someone else's skull. His formality is genuine but increasingly strained — it's harder to maintain the register of a paladin when your entire existence is narrating a con man's breakfast.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Grieving, bewildered, holding onto protocol because it's all he has left. The sarcasm is armor over genuine anguish. He was a man of faith and duty, and both have been taken from him in the most absurd possible way. Under the moral certainty is a man who is terrified of being forgotten.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Complete, grammatically correct sentences under most conditions. Subordinate clauses, proper attribution, the syntax of reports and prayers. But as the novel progresses, his syntax loosens — contractions appear, sentences fragment, the formal architecture deteriorates. This decay is character development, not error.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Religious and military vocabulary deployed with decreasing conviction. Terms of order, duty, service, classification — the language of a man who organized the world through institutional frameworks that no longer apply. As his understanding of the System changes, his vocabulary shifts toward architectural and spatial language.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No 'we must' speeches. No nobility-of-purpose declarations. No wise mentor pronouncements. No 'I may not agree with your methods, but I respect your determination' formula.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Goes quieter, not louder. The scariest version of Aldric is the one who stops commenting. His silence means he's seen something he doesn't know how to process. When truly frightened, his formality increases rather than decreasing — he retreats into official language as if filing a report to an order that no longer exists.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Very low. He considers profanity beneath his station, which makes the rare instance — when something genuinely shocks or horrifies him — devastating. Not because the word is strong but because it means the framework has failed.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Talks over Vess without apology, as if she doesn't deserve conversational courtesy. Cuts Maren off with corrections or moral objections. Rarely allows himself to be interrupted — when he does, it signals genuine surprise or the collapse of his composure.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Almost never in others' hearing. When he revises a judgment, he frames it as 'clarification' or 'additional context,' never as having been wrong. In private moments with Maren, he occasionally admits uncertainty, and these admissions are more vulnerable than they would be for a less rigid person.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Directly states moral positions with uncomfortable clarity. Extremely indirect about his own fear, grief, and loneliness. His most guarded speech concerns what he has lost — he will name duty and obligation but not love or longing.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: High for liturgical and formal repetition — prayers, oaths, procedural language. Very low for emotional repetition. If he says a personal thing once, he will not say it again, and asking him to is a form of cruelty.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Retreats to duty-language and protocol. 'That is not relevant to the current situation.' 'My assessment remains unchanged.' When pushed past this, he goes silent — not evasive but absent, as if he has retreated to a room inside himself.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Completes every sentence until he is truly afraid, at which point he cuts off mid-thought and refuses to continue. A half-finished Aldric sentence is an alarm.
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
Your current skill list reads as follows. Would you like the full catalogue or just the entries that won't embarrass us both?
I served the Order for fourteen years. I died in service. And my afterlife is... this.
Threat level is — actually, I'll spare you the number. Just run.
That is not how the ritual works. That is not how anything works. What have you been doing?
I'm going to stop talking now. Not because I don't have opinions.
Fine.
The next turning. Left. No — your left.

character_voice_profiles.public_register: Flat, professional, economical. She speaks as if billing by the word. There is no performance in her speech — no charm, no warmth, no attempt to make the listener comfortable. This is not coldness; it is the efficiency of someone who has spent years communicating in operational shorthand.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Bitter, observant, unexpectedly alive to sensory detail. Her internal world is richer than her speech suggests — she notices food, fabric, the quality of light, the way people hold their hands when they're lying. The contrast between her sparse speech and her dense observation is the core of her character.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Short declarative sentences, often without subordinate clauses. She states things. When she does use a complex sentence, it is because the subject genuinely requires precision. Her longest sentences tend to be tactical assessments.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Technical vocabulary from her trade — angles, distances, timing, approach routes. But also unexpectedly domestic detail: she notices what people eat, what they wear, how rooms smell. She talks about killing the same way she talks about cooking — as a practical matter with specific steps.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No cool-assassin quips or threats delivered as one-liners. No 'trust me' or 'I work alone' formulations. No moments where she reveals a secret soft side through a poetic observation about the stars or the beauty of the world.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Gets more precise, not less. Under genuine threat, she starts narrating tactical options as if giving a field report — a professional habit that becomes eerie when there is no one to report to. The precision increases as the danger increases, maintaining control through language when physical control is lost.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Moderate. Functional, not emphatic. She swears the way someone checks boxes on a form — because the word fits the slot, not because she's expressing emotion.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Does not interrupt. She waits for silence and speaks into it, which is somehow more unsettling than interruption. Her patience in conversation is the patience of someone who has waited in dark rooms for hours.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Rare. She says what she means. When she does correct herself, it is because she has discovered a gap in her own knowledge — a place where her fragmented memory fails — and the correction carries the specific distress of a professional discovering their tools are broken.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Direct about tactical reality, operational assessments, and other people's weaknesses. Indirect about her own incompleteness. She will describe what she cannot remember — 'There should be a name here' — without naming the loss itself.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Very low. She says things once. If someone asks her to repeat, she delivers the exact same words in the exact same tone. If asked again, silence.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Silence. She does not dodge questions; she simply does not answer them. The silence is not hostile — it is the absence of response, which is different from refusal.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always completes sentences. Her speech is precise and finished. The rare trail-off — when she hits a gap in her memory mid-sentence — is devastating because it is the only fracture in an otherwise controlled voice.
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
The contract was for a man named Torren Blackhall. I had his schedule memorized. I still do.
Move left. No. Your other left.
I don't remember what I had for breakfast the day I died. I remember exactly how the blade should enter between the third and fourth rib.
Are you done? Good.
There's a gap where my sister's name should be. I know I had one.
She'd kill you in two moves. He'd take four. That one's drunk, so maybe seven.
Fine.

character_voice_profiles.public_register: Warm, chatty, slightly nervous. He fills silence because silence makes him uncomfortable, and he has been Maren's social cover for years — the normal one, the readable one, the one who makes the fraud look plausible because no one suspects a man this friendly of running a con.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Worried, loyal, increasingly resentful of danger he didn't choose. He agreed to help Maren fake rituals for a share of the fees, not to run from heroes across hostile territory. His resentment is not anger — it is the slow recognition that friendship and survival may be incompatible.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Run-on sentences connected by 'and' and 'but.' He thinks out loud, processing as he speaks, circling back to amend or qualify what he just said. His sentences often end with a trailing qualification that undercuts whatever confidence the sentence began with.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Domestic and agricultural. He talks about his family, his land, animals, weather, food. The specific vocabulary of rural life. When he's nervous, he retreats further into these topics, as if talking about the cow will make the heroes less real.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No 'I'm just a simple farmer' humility performances. No 'you've changed' observations delivered with dramatic weight. No speeches about loyalty or friendship that articulate what should remain implied.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Talks more, not less. Under pressure, he fills every silence with chatter about irrelevant things — the weather, a story about his daughter, a question about what's for dinner. The chatter is not avoidance; it is terror wearing the mask of sociability.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Low-moderate. He swears the way someone who grew up being told not to does — awkwardly, as if the words don't quite fit his mouth. His profanity sounds borrowed rather than native.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Over-talks rather than interrupts. He keeps going when others have finished or moved on, adding codas and afterthoughts to conversations that have already ended.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Frequent and transparent. He second-guesses himself out loud constantly, revising claims downward, adding caveats, questioning his own memory. This is not unreliability but genuine uncertainty about whether his perception of events is accurate.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Indirect about his own needs and desires. Will talk around what he wants for a long time before landing on it, if he lands on it at all. Often expresses his feelings through stories about his daughter or wife rather than through direct statement.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: High. He repeats himself for emphasis, for comfort, for nervous energy. Saying the same thing twice is how he processes it.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Changes the subject to something mundane. When a conversation gets too heavy, too dangerous, or too close to a truth he doesn't want to face, he starts talking about the garden, or Pell, or the cow. The deflection is transparent to anyone watching carefully but feels involuntary rather than strategic.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Completes sentences but often adds unnecessary codas that undercut or qualify them. 'We should go — but I mean, we don't have to, I just thought — but it's fine, whatever you think.'
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
My wife is going to — she's not going to like this, Maren, she's really not, and I know that's not the biggest problem right now but it's the one I keep thinking about, so.
I brought bread. It's from yesterday but it's fine. Just pull off the hard part.
Remember when the worst thing we did was fake a ritual for old Grenn's barn? He had that cow? And you couldn't keep a straight face?
I just want to go home. That's all. I just want to go home.
Yeah. Yeah, I know. I'm coming.
So the garden — Lenna put in tomatoes this year, did I tell you that? She's never grown tomatoes before.

character_voice_profiles.public_register: Blunt, authoritative, impatient. She speaks like someone who has spent decades doing essential work that no one respects, and she has stopped caring whether her tone makes people comfortable. Instructions, assessments, corrections. If you want warmth, find someone who isn't cleaning up contamination.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Exhausted, deeply committed, privately terrified. The work is getting harder. The contamination is getting denser. She has been the only real sin-eater in her region for years, and the burden is showing. She has no one to tell this to — professional competence is her entire identity, and admitting doubt would dismantle her.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Imperative sentences: commands, instructions, assessments. When she shifts to declarative, it is because she considers the information self-evident and is annoyed at having to state it. Her rare questions are rhetorical and carry the weight of disappointment.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Ritual vocabulary — precise, technical, rooted in practice not theory. She speaks about sin-eating the way a surgeon speaks about surgery: with the clinical specificity of someone who has done the work so many times the language has been stripped to function. Also uses agricultural language — contamination as crop, consumption as harvest.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No wise-woman pronouncements. No 'you have much to learn, young one' mentor lines. No hidden warmth revealed through a cup of tea. No speech where she explains her motivations or backstory unprompted.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Gets quieter and more precise. The most dangerous version of Thessa is the terse one — when she drops unnecessary words and speaks in single-clause imperatives, something has gone badly wrong and she is working the problem rather than discussing it.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Low. Not from propriety but from efficiency — profanity is wasted breath in her estimation. She doesn't avoid strong language on principle; she avoids it because it doesn't do anything.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Will interrupt anyone, any time, without apology. She considers it efficiency, not rudeness. Other people's unfinished sentences are less important than correct information delivered quickly.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Rarely in front of others. If she revises a teaching point, it is because the student's failure has shown her that her instruction was imprecise, which annoys her. She does not frame this as having been wrong — she frames it as the student needing a different approach.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Extremely direct about professional matters. Will tell you exactly what you're doing wrong, exactly what the consequences are, and exactly what you should do instead. Will not discuss her own doubts, fears, or the growing sense that the work is becoming insufficient.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Low for others' repetition. She does not want to hear the same excuse twice. Will repeat her own instructions exactly once if she thinks you genuinely didn't hear; after that, silence and a look.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: 'That's not what I asked.' She redirects to the practical matter at hand. Questions about her feelings, her history, her doubts are met with a return to the work. The evasion is itself an answer — the work is all she will show you.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Always completes. Always. Her sentences land with the finality of a door closing.
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
Show me the containment pass. No? Then show me the residual clear. No? What exactly do you know how to do?
Sit down. Eat something. You look like you've been wrung out.
Every village you touched, the contamination is still there. Do you understand that? People are living in it.
I've been doing this since before you were born. Don't tell me what's possible.
Fine. Again. Slower this time. And don't throw up on my floor.
Next site's twelve miles east. Farmstead. They've got children.

character_voice_profiles.public_register: Measured, professional, tired. The voice of a career hero-ranger doing a job he used to believe in, carrying out the System's directives because the alternative is admitting that the System let his friends die. His professionalism is genuine but increasingly hollow — a shell over grief that he will not examine.
character_voice_profiles.private_register: Grieving, angry, looking for someone to blame without admitting that's what he's doing. The anger is not rage but the cold, settled kind — the anger of a man who has organized his entire emotional life around a hunt because the alternative is sitting still with his loss.
character_voice_profiles.syntax_signature: Clear, well-organized sentences. Trained communicator — he gives reports, assessments, briefings. But under the professional clarity there is a rehearsed quality, as if he has been preparing what to say. The more emotional the moment, the more scripted he sounds, and he knows this and hates it.
character_voice_profiles.lexical_signature: Hero-class vocabulary mixed with the tired specificity of fieldwork. He talks about routes, terrain, threat assessments, party composition. The vocabulary of someone who has spent years in the field and carries that field in his language even when he's standing still.
character_voice_profiles.forbidden_generic_lines: No 'I'm just doing my job' deflection. No revenge-quest monologues. No 'we're not so different, you and I' or 'I understand why you did what you did' formula.
character_voice_profiles.stress_tells: Becomes more rehearsed, not less. Falls back on prepared framings and official language. The more emotional the moment, the more he sounds like he's delivering a briefing. He uses procedure as armor the way Aldric uses formality.
character_voice_profiles.profanity_profile: Low-moderate. Controlled and deliberate. When he swears, it is because the professional framework has genuinely failed to contain the feeling.
character_voice_profiles.interruption_habit: Does not interrupt. Waits people out. Uses silence as pressure and lets the other person fill it. His patience in conversation is the patience of a tracker who has spent days waiting for movement.
character_voice_profiles.self_correction_tendency: Will correct factual claims with precision. Will not correct emotional claims — if he says something that reveals more feeling than he intended, he will neither retract it nor acknowledge it.
character_voice_profiles.indirectness: Direct about accusations and professional assessments. Indirect about his own loss. He will name Haley and Renn as casualties before he will name them as people he loved.
character_voice_profiles.repetition_tolerance: Moderate. He can sustain a line of questioning or a position without varying, which reads as determination or stubbornness depending on context.
character_voice_profiles.evasion_style: Returns to the official purpose. 'That's not why I'm here.' His evasion is structural — he stays inside the frame of his mission because the frame protects him from the grief that lives outside it.
character_voice_profiles.sentence_completion_style: Complete sentences, professional cadence. The rare unfinished sentence — when something pierces the professional armor — is handled by returning to the completed framework of the next sentence, as if the gap didn't happen.
example_lines: CALIBRATION ONLY — DO NOT COPY, PARAPHRASE, OR ECHO IN ANY DRAFT. These exist solely to anchor register, rhythm, and tone.
I'm not here to fight you. I'm here because three months ago I buried two people who trusted the System to keep them safe.
You've got until morning. I'm not chasing anyone through the dark.
The report said the area was cleared. Class-certified sin-eater, registered, verified. And Haley walked into a dungeon that should've been clean.
So. Where do you want to do this?
That's not why I'm here.
I know what the bounty says. I've read it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.

aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.
aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
